title: Topic, Headers, Alternate, and Exchange-to-Exchange Routing · Inference Brief: Headers Exchange Contract
summary: Routing keys encode unstable JSON, topic wildcards overlap unexpectedly, headers matching trusts absent fields, and unroutable messages vanish because mandatory and alternate behavior are not designed together. Planned artifact: Add and verify the headers exchange contract increment in multi-strategy routing and unroutable-message table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use headers exchanges only with typed bounded metadata and explicit all-versus-any matching, not as a schema-free routing escape hatch.
Enumerate overlapping topic bindings and prove which queues receive one copy even when multiple bindings on the same queue match.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Headers Exchange Contract · Theory Headers Exchange Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 6569 in a high-volume telemetry route with one hot customer key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify primary, alternate, returned, and invalid routing outcomes; keep reconnection and retry work cancellable under one deadline; then exhaust the retry age before the attempt count reaches its maximum. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
